---
title: "Gradebook — Research in Finance"
subtitle: "Winter 2025/2026"
format:
  dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    nav-buttons:
      - icon: file-earmark-spreadsheet
        href: grades.csv
        text: "Download CSV"
---

```{python}
#| label: load
#| echo: false

import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

csv_path = Path("grades.csv")
if csv_path.exists():
    df = pd.read_csv(csv_path)
else:
    df = pd.DataFrame(columns=["student_id", "name", "group", "problem_set", "referee_report", "total", "grade"])
```

# Overview

## Row {height=20%}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Students enrolled"
{
    "icon": "people",
    "color": "primary",
    "value": int(df.shape[0])
}
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Mean total"
{
    "icon": "graph-up",
    "color": "secondary",
    "value": (f"{df['total'].mean():.1f}" if "total" in df.columns and df["total"].notna().any() else "—")
}
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Pass rate"
{
    "icon": "check-circle",
    "color": "success",
    "value": (f"{(df['grade'].astype(str).str.replace(',', '.').astype(float) <= 4.0).mean()*100:.0f}%"
              if "grade" in df.columns and df["grade"].notna().any() else "—")
}
```

## Row {height=80%}

### Distribution of total scores {.tabset}

#### Histogram

```{python}
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

if "total" in df.columns and df["total"].notna().any():
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))
    ax.hist(df["total"].dropna(), bins=10, color="#7D9AAA", edgecolor="#575756")
    ax.set_xlabel("Total score (0–100)")
    ax.set_ylabel("Students")
    ax.spines[["top", "right"]].set_visible(False)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
else:
    print("No data yet — populate grades.csv to see the distribution.")
```

#### Table

```{python}
#| echo: false
df
```

# Per assignment

## Row

### Problem set (50%)

```{python}
#| echo: false
if "problem_set" in df.columns and df["problem_set"].notna().any():
    df[["name", "group", "problem_set"]].sort_values("problem_set", ascending=False)
else:
    print("Problem-set column is empty — fill grades.csv to populate.")
```

### Referee report (50%)

```{python}
#| echo: false
if "referee_report" in df.columns and df["referee_report"].notna().any():
    df[["name", "referee_report"]].sort_values("referee_report", ascending=False)
else:
    print("Referee-report column is empty — fill grades.csv to populate.")
```
